ModuleName: 4 - Modules, File I/O, and Error Handling

Artifacts.Name: Module Topics
Artifacts.Description: A structured outline of the module's topics covering Python modules (importing, custom modules, standard library), file I/O using the with statement, and error handling, used by instructors to plan lessons and by learners to preview what will be taught.

Artifacts.Name: Interactive HTML
Artifacts.Description: An interactive lesson page explaining Python exception handling with try-except, covering what exceptions are, how to catch and inspect them, and how to raise them, used by learners to study error handling concepts with in-page explanations and examples.

Artifacts.Name: HTML Document
Artifacts.Description: A practice problem asking learners to read a short Python division script, identify the line that raises an exception, name the exception type (e.g., ZeroDivisionError or ValueError), and explain why it occurs — used as a formative exercise to recognize and diagnose runtime errors.

Artifacts.Name: Interactive HTML
Artifacts.Description: An interactive lesson page introducing Python modules, covering what a module is, how to import them, how to create custom modules, and how the Python path works, used by learners to read and interact with foundational module concepts.

Artifacts.Name: Interactive HTML
Artifacts.Description: An interactive lesson page covering file I/O using Python's with statement, explaining file modes, the open() function, reading methods, and path handling, used by learners to study how to safely and reliably work with files in Python.

Artifacts.Name: Interactive HTML
Artifacts.Description: An interactive lesson page on Python exception handling with try-except, covering runtime exceptions, how to catch and inspect them, and how to raise them, used by learners as a readable reference for writing robust error-handling code.

Artifacts.Name: HTML Document
Artifacts.Description: A list of learning objectives for the module covering Python file I/O, error handling, and modules, outlining the skills learners are expected to demonstrate such as using file modes, the with statement, read/write methods, and exception handling — used by instructors to frame the module and by learners to gauge expected outcomes.

Artifacts.Name: HTML Document
Artifacts.Description: A curated reference list of external resources including the official Python documentation on file I/O and Real Python tutorials, providing learners with authoritative reading material to supplement instruction on opening files, file modes, the with statement, and reading and writing text files.

Artifacts.Name: Van der Waals Gas Analysis: Evaluating Equation-of-State Models with Real Experimental Data
Artifacts.Description: A 2–4 hour problem-solving assignment in which learners write Python code to apply the van der Waals equation of state to real experimental gas data, practising modules, file I/O, and error handling while comparing model predictions to measured values, submitted as a Colab notebook or written report.
